markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Implement Semantic Catalog as backend-indexed routing contracts, not prompt-only helper text. This MT belongs to RetrievalContextAndRanking. Retrieve project knowledge through explainable direct, graph, hybrid, and passage fallback modes.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Retrieval must choose explicit modes: none, direct_load, exact_lookup, graph_traversal, hybrid_rag, or passage_fallback, with non_hybrid_reason where applicable.
Context bundles must be bounded, cited, explainable, restartable, and inspectable through backend and visual-debug surfaces.
scope.expected_behavior: SemanticCatalogBridge leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: SemanticCatalogBridge is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified 1d1f949d; RetrievalContextAndRanking: 46 unit + 10 real-PG tests pass; cheapest-authoritative planner records mode+skip-reason in replayable RetrievalTrace; reuses MT-060 trace/bundle substrate (1 new migration 0260); MT-129 additive QueryRetrievalMode extension; folded-stub bridges (139-142) preserve ProjectBrain/SemanticCatalog/AIReady/ContextPack intent as backend contracts.

evidence.finding: QueryPlan routes hardcoded; the unit-tested catalog->RouteStep bridge (routing_for/route_steps_from_entry) was never called by the planner (spec MUST violation)
evidence.fix: RetrievalRequest.catalog_entry (+ builder); planner resolves routing_for for graph-traversal and hybrid branches - resolvable contracts DERIVE plan.route (steps cite entry+version), unknown/empty entries fall back to defaults (spec C)
evidence.tests: knowledge_retrieval_pipeline_tests 10 passed incl. planner_derives_route_from_semantic_catalog_entry (hybrid + graph catalog-derived routes, unknown-entry default fallback)

findings.title: QueryPlan routes are not derived from the SemanticCatalog
findings.evidence: The bridge docstring (semantic_catalog.rs:7-8) cites spec MUST: QueryPlan.route[] derived from the SemanticCatalog when present. But CheapestAuthoritativePathPlanner builds hardcoded routes (planner.rs steps 3 and 5) and never calls routing_for / route_steps_from_entry. The catalog is queryable + listed in the debug API, but no plan derives its route from it.
findings.fix: Have the planner resolve routing_for(workspace, target) and use route_steps_from_entry to populate plan.route when a catalog entry exists; fall back to defaults otherwise.

claim_mismatches.claim: QueryPlan.route[] MUST be derived from the SemanticCatalog when present
claim_mismatches.reality: the catalog->RouteStep bridge exists and is unit-tested, but the planner ignores it and emits fixed routes

missing_tests: test: a planned query whose target has a catalog entry derives its route from the catalog

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-140 requires the SemanticCatalog implemented as a backend-indexed routing contract whose entries derive QueryPlan.route[] when present (spec 2.6.6.7.14.6 A). The code implements this end-to-end against real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL: the planner resolves a named catalog entry and derives the route, falling back to spec-C defaults otherwise, with real-PG test proof.
findings: Prior adversarial-v2 MEDIUM finding (planner emitted hardcoded routes, never called the catalog bridge) is now actually fixed in commit 06bb2cec.

planner.rs:317-339 adds async catalog_route() which calls semantic_catalog::routing_for(pool, workspace_id, name, max_candidates); both the graph-neighborhood branch (planner.rs ~235-260) and the hybrid_rag branch (~281-310) now derive plan.route from the catalog entry when resolvable, falling back to defaults when the entry is None/unknown/empty.

semantic_catalog.rs:68-83 routing_for resolves via storage::resolve_semantic_catalog_entry against real PostgreSQL table knowledge_semantic_catalog_entries; route_steps_from_entry (lines 38-55) maps catalog query_routes to RetrievalStore steps that cite the catalog entry name+version, satisfying explainability.

Real authority backing: migration 0260_knowledge_semantic_catalog.sql creates knowledge_semantic_catalog_entries with FK to workspaces, JSONB query_routes/selectors, lifecycle_state, and registers it as authority-class in knowledge_schema_registry (MT-140). storage/knowledge_retrieval.rs:793-814 resolve_semantic_catalog_entry runs real SQL (WHERE workspace_id/name/lifecycle='active' ORDER BY version DESC).

Spec MUST confirmed in CURRENT Master Spec v02.194 02-system-architecture.md:11514-11521 (A: QueryPlan.route[] MUST be derived from SemanticCatalog if present) and default policy at line 11544 (spec C) - implementation matches both branches exactly.

Real-PG proof: tests/knowledge_retrieval_pipeline_tests.rs:459-539 planner_derives_route_from_semantic_catalog_entry uses MemoryFixture::setup against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL (knowledge_memory_fixtures.rs pool_for -> PgPoolOptions::connect(schema_url)), upserts a real catalog entry, asserts the hybrid route IS the 2-step catalog contract citing 'loom_neighborhood', graph mode also catalog-derived, and an unknown entry falls back to the 4-step default hybrid route. skip_if_no_pg! only SKIPS when PG is unavailable; it does not substitute a mock.

No placeholder/deferred escapes (todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/Unavailable/'not yet wired'/'pending MT-') in semantic_catalog.rs or planner.rs. Planner is wired into the live execution path at executor.rs:102-103 (planner.plan(request)), so the catalog-derived route reaches runtime candidate generation, not orphaned code.
Aside (non-blocking, paperwork): MT-140 also appears in an unrelated WP-KERNEL-005 commit d2ad663c; not relevant to this WP-009 acceptance.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
